# UC-13.1.34 ¬∑ Real-Time Search Resource Consumption (_internal)

## Description

Real-time searches reserve cores and memory; runaway RT jobs degrade interactive and scheduled workload.

## Value

Real-time searches reserve cores and memory; runaway RT jobs degrade interactive and scheduled workload.

## Implementation

Baseline RT search counts per SH. Alert on unusual RT concurrency. Identify dashboards or users with many concurrent RT panels.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` `group=search_concurrency` / `search_process`.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Baseline RT search counts per SH. Alert on unusual RT concurrency. Identify dashboards or users with many concurrent RT panels.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=search_concurrency
| stats max(active_rt_searches) as rt_active, max(active_hist_searches) as hist by host
| eval rt_ratio=if((rt_active+hist)>0, round(100*rt_active/(rt_active+hist),1), null())
| where rt_active > 20 OR rt_ratio > 40
```

Understanding this SPL

**Real-Time Search Resource Consumption (_internal)** ‚ÄĒ Real-time searches reserve cores and memory; runaway RT jobs degrade interactive and scheduled workload.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` `group=search_concurrency` / `search_process`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ `eval` defines or adjusts **rt_ratio** ‚ÄĒ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where rt_active > 20 OR rt_ratio > 40` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Run the search over calm and busy windows. Compare with the Splunk monitoring console and relevant health and introspection views, and spot-check key fields in sample events.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (RT vs historical searches), Table (hosts with high RT).

## SPL

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=search_concurrency
| stats max(active_rt_searches) as rt_active, max(active_hist_searches) as hist by host
| eval rt_ratio=if((rt_active+hist)>0, round(100*rt_active/(rt_active+hist),1), null())
| where rt_active > 20 OR rt_ratio > 40
```

## Visualization

Line chart (RT vs historical searches), Table (hosts with high RT).
